title: Root Command Surface Audit
goal: Implement #486 by classifying every root command by daily-operation role and adding guard coverage so non-core or source-checkout-only concepts do not become ordinary host-repo command surface.
non_goals: Do not remove or rename commands in this slice.
Do not split cli.py responsibilities; #488 owns extraction.
Do not replace first-party feature tiers with generic module manifests; #490/#510 own that work.

intent.outcome: The root command manifest explains why each command belongs in the shipped host-repo command surface.
intent.constraints: Stay on the current branch; preserve existing CLI behavior; keep classification declarative and low-residue.
intent.latitude: Add manifest fields, schema validation, and tests; adjust compact report/default routing only if a command is currently advertised incorrectly.
intent.escalation: Stop before removing commands, creating a new public command, or moving behavior into modules.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, commit, close #486, refresh external evidence, then promote the next module-boundary-compression child.
execution.proof: Run contract tooling checks, focused workspace CLI tests for command manifest classification, full workspace CLI tests if report/default behavior changes, ruff, proof selector, and planning closeout checks.

invariants: Existing command names, options, and generated adapter parity remain unchanged.
Root command classifications are declarative metadata, not a new execution path.
No source-checkout-only maintainer/dogfooding command is advertised as an ordinary host-repo command.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Keep the root orchestrator thin, boring, generic, and strictly bounded.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #486 closes, reingest and promote the next module-boundary-compression child.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Command-surface role metadata gives later module-manifest and cli.py extraction work a concrete map.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Command removal/demotion, root dependency changes, cli.py splitting, and MCP-style module component design.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Contract/schema checks and command manifest tests.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #487 or #488 depending on refreshed issue priority.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Audit root command surface for daily-operation necessity.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make the root command surface explainable and prevent source-checkout-only or module-owned concepts from becoming first-line host-repo workflow.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add schema-backed role classification to the command manifest and tests that classify all root commands.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Review whether the categories are useful and whether any command should become a future removal/demotion follow-up.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: CLI command manifest, its schema, focused tests, and planning surfaces.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: contract tooling check, focused test for command classifications, workspace CLI tests if command behavior changes, ruff, proof selector, planning checks.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Stop before changing parser behavior or public command names.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Generated packages, command execution code, root dependency footprint, or module manifest redesign.

stop_conditions.stop when: Every root command and prompt subcommand has an explicit role/audience classification and tests guard the classification.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A command appears unjustifiable and should be hidden or removed instead of merely classified.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The work becomes command removal, module ownership transfer, or cli.py extraction.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Manifest parity or generated-command checks fail due role metadata.

context_budget.recoverable later: Full root CLI implementation and module-manifest redesign.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update this execplan if a removal/demotion follow-up is discovered.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Not needed unless compact output points to Memory.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Role-classification slice only; do not alter command behavior.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Parser behavior changes, command removal, or broad module manifest redesign.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Classify the root command surface for #486.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Preserve existing CLI behavior and stay on current branch.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact role names and tests, provided they map to the issue categories.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Classification shows a command should be hidden/removed or owned by a module now.

active_milestone.scope: Add command role classification metadata and tests.
active_milestone.ready: #486 is the selected next child for #485.

immediate_next_action: Archive this plan, commit, close #486, refresh external evidence, then promote the next module-boundary-compression child.
invariants: Source-checkout-only work is not advertised as a root host-repo command.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Root command role classification lives in cli_commands.json until a later module-manifest model supersedes it.
open_questions_to_close: Whether any command should be routed as future module-owned or hide/remove follow-up.

validation_commands.command: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "root_command_manifest_classifies or modules_command_lists"
uv run ruff check src tests

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed src/agentic_workspace/contracts/cli_commands.json src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/cli_commands.schema.json tests/test_workspace_cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/root-command-surface-audit.plan.json --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json

completion_criteria: Every root command has a role classification.
Source-checkout-only or module-owned candidates are identified.
First-contact docs/defaults are not expanded to advertise non-core commands.

execution_run.what happened: Added schema-backed role, audience, and classification-note metadata to every root command and root subcommand in cli_commands.json.
execution_run.scope touched: Command manifest/schema, focused workspace CLI test coverage, and planning surfaces.
execution_run.changed surfaces: The command manifest now classifies core lifecycle, core context/router, reusable host-repo diagnostics, module front doors, local-only calibration, and absence of source-checkout-only command roles.
execution_run.validations run: Contract tooling check, focused command-classification test, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning-surface check, proof selector, and planning doctor.
execution_run.result for continuation: #486 is ready to archive and close; #487, #488, #490, and #510-#514 remain under #485.
execution_run.next step: Archive plan, commit, push, close #486, refresh external evidence, then promote the next child.

finished_run_review.config compliance: used package workflow surfaces for report, summary, proof, doctor, reconcile, and planning closeout

proof_report.validation proof: full workspace CLI tests, ruff, contract tooling, planning surfaces, proof selector, and planning doctor passed
proof_report.proof achieved now: Every root command and prompt/external-intent subcommand has schema-backed role/audience metadata and tests guard the shipped host-repo boundary.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: cli_commands.json classifies every root command by role/audience and the focused test asserts no source-checkout-only or remove/hide roles are shipped as ordinary commands.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Root orchestrator should be thin, generic, host-repo useful, and low-residue.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Classify and guard before removing or redesigning.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this make the root command surface easier to explain and review?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded slice is complete and remaining intent is routed to a checked-in continuation owner.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Prepared closeout records intent satisfaction, closure decision, proof evidence, and distillation buckets.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen when .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane module-boundary-compression activates a fresh bounded slice.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Root command surface has explicit schema-backed command roles and audiences.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused and full workspace CLI tests, contract tooling, ruff, proof selector, planning surfaces, and planning doctor passed.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): No Memory/docs/config promotion needed; command manifest is the durable owner.
execution_summary.resume from: Reingest open issues and promote the next #485 child.

discard.summary: Command-by-command execution detail stays in the archived proof record.

memory.summary: No Memory/docs/config promotion needed; command manifest is the durable owner.

config_check.summary: No Memory/docs/config promotion needed; command manifest is the durable owner.

docs.summary: No Memory/docs/config promotion needed; command manifest is the durable owner.
